currentChartValues.pain_assessment_narrative: Generalized pain with a scale 5/10; Comfort measures and pain medication were provided. Re-checked after 30 minutes, resulting with good relief.

extractedFieldValues.current_value: Edward Clyde Johnson, a 74-year-old male with a complex medical history, is being referred for home health care following a recent hospitalization and surgery for a right reverse total shoulder arthroplasty (TSA). His diagnosis includes severe osteoarthritis of the right shoulder, along with hypertension, diabetes, dyslipidemia, coronary artery disease with prior percutaneous coronary intervention and stenting, and hypothyroidism. The elective procedure was performed due to longstanding shoulder pain and functional limitations that did not improve with outpatient conservative treatment. He now requires skilled home health services to support his recovery.
